description: Bot 6 levels: IBH SELL only + FIB_0.236 cap=18 DEPLOYED

changes_from_previous.added_ibh_sell: Re-included IBH with SELL-only direction filter (80.8% WR, +$4.05/day)
changes_from_previous.increased_0236_cap: FIB_0.236 cap 12→18 (WR improves with more tests, 84% at #6-8)
changes_from_previous.rationale: Offensive approach: ADD profitable trades instead of cutting. Combined +$6.97/day vs previous baseline.

experiments: direction_filters (20 variants): IBH SELL +$4.24/day, FIB_0.764 BUY lowest MaxDD
scoring_filters (13 variants): 30s global cooldown +$0.37/day, no-reverse/tick-rate hurt
tick_rate_sweep (10 variants): every threshold costs P&L, high tick rate is mix of good/bad
bot_scoring (10 variants): scoring with old and fresh weights both hurt P&L at 1pt entry
walkforward_scoring: trained fresh weights on 200 days, tested 132 held-out — still hurts
fib618_tuning (9 variants): removing FIB_0.618 doubles MaxDD, cap=3 is optimal
risk_management (12 variants): trend/VWAP/confirmation/split-budget all hurt P&L
reactive_risk (9 variants): $75 limit -$2.21/day but cuts bad days 48→21
confirmation (6+10 variants): level confirmation reduces bad days but costs $1-17/day
smart_limits (9 variants): $75 conditional limit -$1.37/day, $60 first hour -$1.41/day
offensive_combos (8 variants): IBH SELL + 0.236 cap=18 = +$6.97/day DEPLOYED
defensive_v2 (10 variants): tested on new deployed config, none beat baseline P&L
first_hour_v1 (8 variants): adaptive caps deployed — half caps first 30 min, restore on 3 wins, extend on loss
key_insights: Scoring does not work at 1pt entry level — WR flat across all factor buckets
Defensive filters always cost P&L — conditions that predict bad days also occur on good days
Offensive approach (add trades) works better than defensive (cut trades)
FIB_0.236 WR improves with more tests (87% at #6-8), higher cap adds P&L
IBH SELL only is +$4.05/day at 80.8% WR, BUY side drags it down
After first loss on bad days: 0% recovery. On good days: 86% recovery
Bad days trade 2x faster (14.3/hr vs 6.4/hr), 72% of losses in first hour
Defensive v2: IBH cap=3 best MaxDD ($451) at -$2.47/day; $60 1hr limit halves bad days (57→33) at -$1.56/day
Defensive v2: all combos cost $6-7/day — stacking filters has diminishing returns
Decision: keep baseline as-is, defensive filters not worth the P&L cost
First-hour analysis: bad days lose within 7 min of IB (median), 1.3 wins before first loss vs 4.6 good days
Adaptive caps deployed: half caps 30 min after IB, restore on 3 wins, extend on loss. $38.79/day, 38 bad days (vs 57)
Human-vs-bot analysis (2026-05-01): human scoring weights don't transfer to bot. WR flat across all score buckets at 1pt entry.
Streak filter would HURT: after 2+L, bot WR is 84.5% (+$775). Recovery on good days (95.3%) far outweighs bad-day losses.
Counter-trend vs with-trend: identical WR (79.5% vs 79.3%). Direction doesn't predict outcomes.
Bot's 79% WR at 1pt is structural — no scoring factor differentiates winners from losers.
